Question: Define a point.
Answer: A point is an exact location in space. It has no length, width, or thickness and is represented by a dot.

Question: Define a line.
Answer: A line is a straight one-dimensional figure that extends infinitely in both directions. It has length but no breadth or thickness.

Question: Define a line segment.
Answer: A line segment is part of a line with two endpoints. It has a definite length.

Question: Define a ray.
Answer: A ray is part of a line that has one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction.

Question: Define a plane.
Answer: A plane is a flat two-dimensional surface that extends infinitely in all directions.

Question: Differentiate between intersecting and parallel lines.
Answer: Intersecting lines meet at a point, whereas parallel lines never meet.

Question: Draw and define a triangle.
Answer: A triangle is a polygon with three sides and three angles. The sum of its angles is 180 degrees.

Question: Explain different types of triangles based on sides.
Answer: Equilateral triangle: all sides equal; Isosceles triangle: two sides equal; Scalene triangle: all sides unequal.

Question: Explain different types of triangles based on angles.
Answer: Acute triangle: all angles < 90°; Right triangle: one angle = 90°; Obtuse triangle: one angle > 90°.

Question: Define a quadrilateral and give examples.
Answer: A quadrilateral is a polygon with four sides and four angles. Examples: square, rectangle, trapezium, parallelogram.

Question: Define a polygon.
Answer: A polygon is a closed plane figure made up of three or more straight line segments.

Question: Differentiate between regular and irregular polygons.
Answer: Regular polygon: all sides and angles are equal. Irregular polygon: sides or angles are unequal.

Question: Define a circle.
Answer: A circle is a set of points in a plane equidistant from a fixed point called the center.

Question: Define radius and diameter of a circle.
Answer: Radius is the distance from the center to any point on the circle. Diameter is a line passing through the center, joining two points on the circle; diameter = 2 × radius.

Question: Define chord and tangent.
Answer: Chord: a line segment joining two points on a circle. Tangent: a line that touches a circle at exactly one point.

Question: Define concentric circles.
Answer: Two or more circles that have the same center but different radii.

Answer: obtuse
column5: Acute: <90°, Right: 90°, Obtuse: >90° but <180°, Straight: 180°, Reflex: >180°

Question: Define complementary and supplementary angles.
Answer: Complementary angles sum to 90°, Supplementary angles sum to 180°.

Question: Explain properties of angles at a point.
Answer: Sum of angles at a point is 360°.

Question: Explain vertically opposite angles.
Answer: When two lines intersect, the angles opposite each other are equal. These are called vertically opposite angles.

Question: Define adjacent angles.
Answer: Angles that have a common vertex and a common side but do not overlap.

Question: Draw and explain a square.
Answer: A square is a quadrilateral with all sides equal and all angles 90°. Properties: diagonals are equal and bisect each other at 90°.

Question: Draw and explain a rectangle.
Answer: A rectangle is a quadrilateral with opposite sides equal and all angles 90°. Diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

Question: Define a parallelogram and list properties.
Answer: A parallelogram is a quadrilateral with opposite sides parallel and equal. Opposite angles are equal; diagonals bisect each other.

Question: Define a rhombus and list properties.
Answer: A rhombus is a quadrilateral with all sides equal and opposite sides parallel. Diagonals bisect at right angles.

Question: Define a trapezium and list properties.
Answer: A trapezium is a quadrilateral with one pair of opposite sides parallel. Non-parallel sides are called legs.

Question: Explain the sum of interior angles of a polygon.
Answer: Sum of interior angles of an n-sided polygon = (n−2)×180°.

Question: Explain the sum of exterior angles of a polygon.
Answer: Sum of exterior angles of a polygon = 360°.

Question: Define a diagonal and give examples.
Answer: "A diagonal is a line joining two non-adjacent vertices of a polygon. Example: AC in square ABCD."

Question: Explain the shortest distance from a point to a line.
Answer: The perpendicular drawn from the point to the line gives the shortest distance.

Question: Define hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle.
Answer: The side opposite the right angle in a right triangle is the hypotenuse; it is the longest side.

Question: Explain the relation between sides of a triangle.
Answer: In any triangle, the sum of lengths of any two sides is greater than the third side.

Question: Define the mid-point of a line segment.
Answer: The point which divides a line segment into two equal parts is called the mid-point.

Question: Define bisector of a line segment.
Answer: A line or segment that divides a line segment into two equal parts is called its bisector.

Question: Define angle bisector.
Answer: A ray or line that divides an angle into two equal parts is called an angle bisector.

Question: Draw a right-angled triangle and label sides and angles.
Answer: A triangle with one 90° angle; sides: base, height, hypotenuse; angles: right angle, other two angles.

Question: Draw an isosceles triangle and explain properties.
Answer: Triangle with two equal sides; angles opposite equal sides are equal.

Question: Draw an equilateral triangle and explain properties.
Answer: Triangle with all sides equal; all angles 60°.

Question: Draw a scalene triangle and explain properties.
Answer: Triangle with all sides unequal; all angles unequal.

Question: Draw an obtuse triangle and explain properties.
Answer: Triangle with one angle > 90°; sum of angles is 180°.

Question: Draw an acute triangle and explain properties.
Answer: Triangle with all angles < 90°; sum of angles is 180°.

Question: Define a secant of a circle.
Answer: A line that intersects a circle at two points.

Question: Define tangent of a circle and state a property.
Answer: A line that touches the circle at exactly one point; perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

Question: Explain concentric and non-concentric circles.
Answer: Concentric circles share the same center; non-concentric circles have different centers.

Question: Explain the properties of diagonals of a rectangle.
Answer: Diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

Question: Explain the properties of diagonals of a square.
Answer: Diagonals are equal, bisect each other at 90°, and bisect angles.

Question: Explain the properties of diagonals of a rhombus.
Answer: Diagonals bisect each other at 90° and bisect the angles of the rhombus.

Question: Explain the difference between regular and irregular polygons.
Answer: Regular polygon: all sides and angles equal. Irregular polygon: sides or angles unequal.

Question: Define a geometric figure.
Answer: "A geometric figure is a set of points
column4: and curves arranged in a particular pattern or shape."

Question: Explain the difference between open and closed figures.
Answer: Open figure: does not enclose a space (e.g., line segment, ray). Closed figure: encloses space (e.g., triangle, square).

Question: Explain the difference between convex and concave polygons.
Answer: Convex polygon: all interior angles <180°; Concave polygon: at least one interior angle >180°.

Question: Explain how to construct a perpendicular bisector of a line segment.
Answer: Using a compass, draw arcs from both endpoints with radius > half the segment; join intersection points to get perpendicular bisector.

Question: Explain how to construct the bisector of an angle.
Answer: Using a compass, draw arcs from vertex and points on sides; draw arcs from these points; join intersection to vertex for angle bisector.
